task:
Add a PERSISTED PER-REPO "Foreman engagement" setting so Foreman engages by default in EVERY project — including fresh non-git projects — and stops silently disengaging when a directory has no .git. This edits the Foreman extension at extensions/foreman/. The founder has already decided the design; implement exactly this, do not redesign.

BACKGROUND (why): the route-through-Foreman guard currently no-ops whenever findGitRoot() returns null (non-git dir), so in a fresh project like ~/Desktop/Oscar/ClipLab the guard never fires and the agent hand-edits everything outside Foreman. We want Foreman ON by default everywhere; in a non-git project the FIRST real-work attempt should be gated so the CTO can ask the founder whether to `git init` + use Foreman, or disable Foreman for that repo (a choice that then PERSISTS and is shown on the status line). A pure "quick questions" session performs no mutations, so it must stay completely silent (nothing asked, nothing written).

KEY CONSTRAINT discovered from the pi type defs (honor it): the `session_start` event handler returns void — it CANNOT open a blocking question/modal. It may only set status / notify. Therefore the ASK happens at the first-mutation guard→CTO path (a charter behavior), and `session_start` only paints a passive status reminder.

DESIGN (locked):
- engagement resolution = persisted repo setting → default ON. (No separate session-only layer; /foreman-direct now PERSISTS per-repo, per founder decision.)
- Persistence = a SINGLE out-of-tree JSON map at the agent dir: `<agentDir>/foreman/engagement.json`, where agentDir resolves from process.env.PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR else ~/.pi/agent. Only OFF overrides are stored (default ON = key absent). NEVER write anything into the target project for this (a declined/quick-questions dir stays pristine). One file scales to all repos.
- Decline options are BINARY: [Init git + Foreman] or [Disable Foreman for this repo].
- /foreman-direct is UNIFIED with this: toggling it OFF persists OFF for the repo; toggling ON clears the persisted override (back to default ON).

IMPLEMENTATION (mirror existing module style; engagement.ts must be PURE node/fs with NO pi imports, exactly like gates.ts/planner.ts, so it is headlessly unit-testable):

A. NEW FILE extensions/foreman/engagement.ts:
   - Resolve the store path: `engagementStorePath()` returns process.env.FOREMAN_ENGAGEMENT_STORE if set (this is the test seam), else path.join(agentDir, "foreman", "engagement.json") where agentDir = process.env.PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR?.trim() || path.join(os.homedir(), ".pi", "agent"). (Mirror how ledger.ts resolves its mirror root from env.)
   - A stable per-repo key: `repoEngagementKey(root)` = path.resolve(root) sanitized tail + short sha256 hash (you may copy the small repoKey pattern from ledger.ts; keep engagement.ts self-contained — do NOT import ledger.ts).
   - Store shape: `{ "repos": { "<key>": { "active": false, "path": "<resolved root>", "updatedAt": "<iso>" } } }`. Tolerant reader: missing file / bad JSON / wrong shape => treat as empty (everything default ON). Never throw.
   - `readRepoEngagement(root): { active: boolean; source: "default" | "repo" }` — returns { active: true, source: "default" } when no override stored; { active: false, source: "repo" } when an OFF override exists. (Only OFF is ever stored, so any stored entry means active:false.)
   - `repoEngagementActive(root): boolean` — convenience = readRepoEngagement(root).active.
   - `setRepoEngagement(root, active: boolean): void` — if active===false, write/merge the OFF override (with path + updatedAt) and persist; if active===true, DELETE that repo's key (back to default ON) and persist. mkdir -p the store dir. Best-effort but must persist correctly; tolerate concurrent/missing dirs.
   - Keep it small and pure; no pi import; node builtins only (fs, os, path, crypto).

B. extensions/foreman/index.ts — wire it (this file imports pi; that's fine):
   1. Import { repoEngagementActive, setRepoEngagement } from "./engagement.ts".
   2. REPLACE the session-only `directMode` global mechanism with persisted engagement:
      - In the `pi.on("tool_call", ...)` handler: keep the `if (process.env.FOREMAN_CREW === "1") return undefined;` crew bypass. Then compute `const root = findGitRoot(ctx.cwd) ?? ctx.cwd;` and `if (!repoEngagementActive(root)) return undefined;` (OFF persisted => no gating). Then classify with a CWD-FALLBACK repo root so non-git dirs still gate: pass `findRepoRoot: (p) => findGitRoot(p) ?? ctx.cwd` (do NOT modify guard.ts — the fallback is purely what index.ts passes; guard.ts keeps repoRoot===null semantics). Keep scratchDirs as today. If classification.gate is false return undefined.
      - When gating, if the dir is non-git (`findGitRoot(ctx.cwd) === null`), APPEND a git-init-aware note to classification.reason before returning, e.g. a NON_GIT_HINT constant: "This directory is not a git repository. Per charter, ask the founder whether to initialize git here and run this through Foreman, or disable Foreman for this repo with foreman({ engage: false })." Return { block: true, reason }.
   3. Update the `/foreman-direct` registerCommand handler to PERSIST per-repo: compute root = findGitRoot(ctx.cwd) ?? ctx.cwd; const next = !repoEngagementActive(root); setRepoEngagement(root, next). When next===false (now disabled): set the existing DIRECT_STATUS_KEY status to something like "⚠ foreman-direct ON (repo)" and notify that direct edits are allowed AND the choice is persisted for this repo. When next===true (re-enabled): clear that status and notify Foreman is re-enabled for this repo. Update the command description to say it persists per-repo.
   4. Add an optional `engage` boolean to the foreman tool: in LoopParams add `engage: { type: "boolean", description: "Set Foreman engagement for this repo: false disables (persists; direct edits allowed), true re-enables (clears the override). Used by the CTO to record the founder's non-git decline." }`. In execute(), EARLY (before resume/task handling), if `typeof params.engage === "boolean"`: root = findGitRoot(cwd) ?? cwd; setRepoEngagement(root, params.engage); set/clear the DIRECT_STATUS_KEY status via ctx.ui if available; return a short text result explaining the new state and how to reverse it. Do not start a task in that call.
   5. Add a `pi.on("session_start", (_event, ctx) => { ... })` handler: root = findGitRoot(ctx.cwd) ?? ctx.cwd; if (!repoEngagementActive(root)) ctx.ui?.setStatus?.(DIRECT_STATUS_KEY, "⚠ foreman-direct ON (repo)"). Must be safe headless (optional-chain setStatus; never throw). Do NOT ask anything here (the event cannot block).
   6. Remove the now-unused `directMode` variable cleanly (no dead code, no backwards-compat shim) — engagement is now the single source of truth. Make sure no other reference to directMode remains.

C. extensions/foreman/AGENTS.md and extensions/foreman/docs/CHARTER.md — the behavioral fix (this is the point of the task):
   - Document that Foreman is ON by default in EVERY repo, git or not. In a NON-GIT project, the first implementation attempt (edit/write/mutating bash) is gated; when that happens the CTO MUST ask the founder with a single-select AskUserQuestion (binary): "Init git + Foreman" vs "Disable Foreman for this repo". Map the answers: "Init git + Foreman" => run `git init` in the project, then start the work as a normal foreman({ task }) task; "Disable Foreman for this repo" => call foreman({ engage: false }) to persist the OFF choice (direct edits allowed; reversible with foreman({ engage: true }) or /foreman-direct). Note the status line shows "⚠ foreman-direct ON (repo)" while a repo has Foreman disabled, so the choice is remembered across sessions. Keep it advisory/honest: a quick-questions session that changes nothing is never interrupted. Keep these edits concise and in the existing voice; AGENTS.md gets the operating detail, CHARTER.md gets a concise mirror.

D. Tests — extend the ALREADY-WIRED extensions/foreman/test/guard_test.sh (engagement governs the guard, so it is in-domain; this guarantees the existing per-round verify gate exercises the new code WITHOUT needing to change the .pi/foreman.json gate command). Follow the existing headless `node --input-type=module` style in that file. Cover:
   - engagement.ts via a temp store: set process.env.FOREMAN_ENGAGEMENT_STORE to a tmp file path; assert default repoEngagementActive(root) is true (source "default"); after setRepoEngagement(root, false) it is false (source "repo") and persists across a fresh read; after setRepoEngagement(root, true) it returns to default true and the key is removed; different roots are independent; a missing/corrupt store file is tolerated (no throw, default ON).
   - the CWD-FALLBACK gating CONTRACT at the classifyToolCall level (proving the wiring index.ts relies on): with findRepoRoot returning the cwd (simulating findGitRoot()??cwd in a non-git dir), a write to a path UNDER cwd returns gate:true; a write to a path OUTSIDE cwd returns gate:false; a scratch/tmp path returns gate:false; a prose .md under cwd returns gate:false. (Use guard.classifyToolCall with an injected findRepoRoot; do not import index.ts.)
   - Clean up any env var / temp dirs you set so other suites are unaffected (unset FOREMAN_ENGAGEMENT_STORE at the end).

CONSTRAINTS:
- engagement.ts: pure node/fs, no pi import (mirror gates.ts/planner.ts) so guard_test.sh can import it headlessly.
- Do NOT modify guard.ts's repoRoot===null semantics; the cwd fallback is only what index.ts passes as findRepoRoot.
- Never write engagement state into the target project; it lives only in the out-of-tree store.
- No files are written for a session that performs no mutations (verify this property holds: session_start only reads + sets status; it must not create the store).
- Quota safety: do not touch the append-only system-prompt mechanism.
- Remove directMode cleanly; do not leave a compatibility shim.
- The per-round verify gate (the repo's .pi/foreman.json "verify" gate, which runs guard_test.sh among others) must pass, and so must the full suite.
verifyCommand: for t in extensions/foreman/test/*.sh extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/*.sh; do echo "== $t =="; bash "$t" || exit 1; done
